Pre-condition: App is newly installed. SV95 nearby.
Test steps:
1. Launch the App for the first time.
2. Observe the 'Nearby Devices' permission request.
3. Select 'Allow'.
Expected Result: App correctly asks for permission and starts scanning after user allows.

Test steps:
1. Launch the App.
2. Deny 'Nearby Devices' and 'Location' permissions.
3. Navigate to the scan screen.
Expected Result: App shows a clear message and a button to redirect user to system settings.

Test steps:
1. Go to 'Add Device' screen.
2. Wait for scanning to complete.
Expected Result: SV95 is discovered and listed. DFU check no longer blocks discovery.

Test steps:
1. Connect the SV95 device.
2. Inspect the device timestamp or system logs.
Expected Result: App automatically sends 'Set RTC' command to sync clock.

Test steps:
1. Connect SV95.
2. App receives null/default schedule info (1970).
Expected Result: App automatically calls 'Set Schedule' (2s Sampling / 2m Interval / 3y Duration).

Test steps:
1. Turn off SV95 and wait for 'Disconnected'.
2. Turn SV95 back on.
Expected Result: App re-establishes connection automatically within 10 seconds.

Test steps:
1. Navigate to 'Available devices' screen.
2. Observe the device list.
Expected Result: Each SV95 is listed separately with its unique Serial Number and Product Image.

Test steps:
1. Look at the SV95 card on Home screen.
2. Compare values with the physical sensor if possible.
Expected Result: Card displays Overall Acceleration for X, Y, and Z axes correctly.

Test steps:
1. Look at the SV95 card.
2. Locate the temperature reading.
Expected Result: Temperature value is displayed clearly next to the vibration data.

Test steps: 1. Observe the text color of the OA value on the card.
Expected Result: Value turns YELLOW to indicate an Alert state.

Test steps: 1. Observe the text color of the OA value on the card.
Expected Result: Value turns RED to indicate a Danger state.

Test steps: 1. Observe the Home screen device list.
Expected Result: SV95 card layout is consistent with other METERLiNK devices but shows specific OA/Temp fields.

Test steps:
1. Enter SV95 Device Detail screen.
2. Verify the 'Report' button is removed.
3. Observe the Trend Card.
Expected Result: Report button is gone; Trend Card shows historical vibration/temp plots correctly.

Test steps: 1. View the chart in Device Detail.
Expected Result: Chart supports two Y-axes to show Vibration (mm/s) and Temperature (C) at once.

Test steps: 1. Tap the 'Full-screen' icon on the Trend/Live card.
Expected Result: Chart expands to fill the entire screen and supports landscape orientation.

Test steps:
1. Tap on any data point on the chart.
2. Drag the marker along the timeline.
Expected Result: A vertical marker follows the finger and displays exact value/timestamp.

Test steps:
1. Open the filter menu in Trend Card.
2. Select a specific date range.
Expected Result: Trend chart updates to show only the selected data segment.

Test steps:
1. Open the filter menu.
2. Set a minimum vibration value threshold.
Expected Result: Trend chart filters out all data points below the threshold.

Test steps:
1. Navigate to Alarm Settings.
2. Select 'Group 1: Large Machines'.
Expected Result: Thresholds are updated to match ISO 10816 standards for large machines.

Test steps:
1. Navigate to Alarm Settings.
2. Select 'Group 2: Medium Machines'.
Expected Result: Thresholds are updated correctly for medium-sized machines.

Test steps:
1. Manually enter custom Alert and Danger values.
2. Tap 'Save'.
Expected Result: Custom limits are saved and correctly used for color-coding and notifications.

Test steps:
1. Change OA unit to inch/s in More page.
2. Return to Alarm Settings.
Expected Result: The ISO and manual thresholds are correctly converted to the new unit.

Test steps:
1. Tap the 'More' (Settings) icon.
2. Monitor BLE communication.
Expected Result: App sends 'Get Wi-Fi', 'Get MQTT', and 'Get Schedule' commands immediately.

Test steps:
1. Switch OA unit to 'inch/s'.
2. Check Home Card and Charts.
Expected Result: All vibration values across the app are converted correctly.

Test steps:
1. Switch Temp unit to 'F'.
2. Check Home Card and Charts.
Expected Result: Temperature readings convert correctly.

Test title: Unit Switching: Temp Alarm Clear Logic
Test steps:
1. Change Temperature unit (e.g. C to F).
2. Go back to Alarm Settings.
Expected Result: All previous Temperature Alarm thresholds are CLEARED as per App logic.

Test steps: 1. Tap on 'Wi-Fi Settings'.
Expected Result: Sub-page opens and displays the current Wi-Fi SSID and status.

Test steps: 1. Tap on 'MQTT Settings'.
Expected Result: Sub-page opens and displays Broker, Port, and Topic info.

Test steps: 1. Tap on 'Schedule Settings'.
Expected Result: Sub-page opens and displays Sampling, Interval, and Schedule status.

Test steps:
1. On a new SV95, tap 'Apply Wi-Fi settings from existing device'.
2. Select the other SV95.
Expected Result: Wi-Fi credentials are successfully copied to the new device.

Test steps:
1. On a new SV95, tap 'Apply MQTT settings from existing device'.
2. Select the other SV95.
Expected Result: MQTT settings (Broker, Port, etc.) are successfully copied.

Test steps:
1. Tap the 'FILES' tab.
2. Verify 'Gallery' button is not visible.
3. Tap 'Recording List'.
Expected Result: Gallery button is hidden for SV95. Recording List is accessible.

Test steps:
1. Navigate to Recording List.
2. Select a file and tap 'Download'.
Expected Result: File is downloaded successfully and becomes viewable in the local file list.

Test steps:
1. Delete a log from the App list.
2. Choose to 'Erase from Device'.
Expected Result: Log is removed from the App list and the hardware memory.

Test steps: 1. Navigate through all SV95 screens (Home, Detail, More).
Expected Result: UI elements, charts, and text remain legible with correct dark theme colors.

Test steps:
1. Launch App on Tablet.
2. Navigate through SV95 pages.
Expected Result: Layout is functional and orientation changes are handled correctly.

Test steps: 1. Verify targetSdkVersion in build configuration.
Expected Result: App correctly targets Google API Level 36.

Test steps: 1. Verify CVE fixes and signing key.
Expected Result: App passes security requirements and uses the correct signing key (/1338).

Test steps:
1. Trigger a vibration alarm.
2. Observe the push notification.
Expected Result: Notification format: "%Device% measurement %val% is higher than [alert|danger] value %limit%".

Test steps: 1. Cold start the App.
Expected Result: App launch time is within the performance threshold for high-end SDK versions.

Test steps:
1. Change Sampling to 1s and Interval to 1min.
2. Disconnect and reconnect.
Expected Result: The modified schedule settings are correctly saved on the SV95 hardware.

Test steps:
1. Connect SV95.
2. Interrupt connection during 'Get Info' phase.
Expected Result: App handles the interruption gracefully and allows user to retry the connection.

Test steps: 1. Look at the SV95 card on Home screen.
Expected Result: Card shows 'Disconnected' status and grays out the last known values.

Test steps:
1. Enter Device Detail.
2. Observe the Trend Card.
Expected Result: Trend Card shows an 'Empty' or 'No Data' placeholder image/text.

Test steps: 1. Tap 'Wi-Fi Settings'.
Expected Result: App sends 'Get Wi-Fi info' command immediately to ensure latest data.

Test steps: 1. Tap 'MQTT Settings'.
Expected Result: App sends 'Get MQTT info' command immediately.

Test steps: 1. Tap 'Schedule Settings'.
Expected Result: App sends 'Get Schedule info' command immediately.

Test steps:
1. Select 5 files for download.
2. Simulate one file failing.
Expected Result: App reports success for 4 files and shows a clear 'Failed' status for the specific 5th file.

Test steps: 1. Try to connect to the same SV95 from Phone B.
Expected Result: App on Phone B correctly identifies the device is busy or currently unavailable.

Test steps:
1. Navigate to Alarm Settings.
2. Select 'Group 3: Small Machines'.
Expected Result: Thresholds are updated correctly for small machine categories.

Test title: OA Unit Switch - Home Card refresh
Test steps:
1. Switch OA unit.
2. Immediately return to Home screen.
Expected Result: Home card values and units reflect the change instantly.

Test title: Temp Unit Switch - Home Card refresh
Test steps:
1. Switch Temp unit.
2. Immediately return to Home screen.
Expected Result: Home card temperature value and unit reflect the change instantly.

Test steps: 1. Rotate the phone between Portrait and Landscape.
Expected Result: Chart re-scales correctly without resetting the marker or zoom level.

Test steps: 1. Connect SV95.
Expected Result: Verify the order of commands: Set RTC -> Get Wi-Fi -> Get MQTT -> Get Schedule.

Test steps:
1. Use the search bar in Recording List.
2. Filter by date.
Expected Result: App correctly filters the list of device recordings.

Test steps: 1. Try to input 1 min as Interval. 2. Try to input 1440 mins. 3. Try to input 1441 mins.
Expected Result: App blocks 1 min and 1441 mins; 1440 mins is accepted successfully.

Test title: Recording Time Unit and Logic Check
Test steps: 1. Verify the unit of Recording Time. 2. Try to set Recording Time > Interval.
Expected Result: Unit is strictly in Seconds. App prevents saving if Recording Time exceeds the Interval value.

Test steps: 1. Set Alert value to 5.0. 2. Attempt to set Danger value to 4.0. 3. Save.
Expected Result: App blocks the save operation and shows an error message indicating that Danger value must be greater than Alert value.

Test steps: 1. Enter a 5-character passcode. 2. Enter a 9-character passcode. 3. Enter a 7-character alphanumeric passcode.
Expected Result: App blocks 5 and 9 character inputs. Passcode must be 6-8 alphanumeric characters long.

Test steps: 1. Enter 0 as Port. 2. Enter 65536 as Port. 3. Enter 1883 as Port.
Expected Result: App blocks 0 and 65536. Valid range is 1-65535.

Test steps: 1. Tap the "Test" connection button. 2. Immediately try to tap it again.
Expected Result: The Test button becomes disabled or unresponsive for a specified cooldown period after the first tap to prevent spamming.

Test title: Advanced Chart Interaction - Show Marker Toggle
Test steps: 1. Locate and toggle the "Show Marker" switch.
Expected Result: The marker visibility toggles on/off correctly; chart remains interactive.

Test steps: 1. Tap on a specific line (X, Y, Z, or Temp) on the chart.
Expected Result: App displays a tooltip showing the specific value and unit for the selected data line.
